title: Leucas plukenetii
intro: Leucas plukenetii, also known as Indian sage or Kukubal, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Lamiaceae. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its numerous health benefits, including the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory disorders, as well as its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Leucas plukenetii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Leucas plukenetii is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder, and is used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory infections, digestive issues, and skin problems.
taxonomy: Leucas plukenetii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It belongs to the genus Leucas, which is a group of plants commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The plant is a member of the order Lamiales and the class Magnoliopsida. Within the kingdom Plantae, Leucas plukenetii is placed alongside other flowering plants. Its classification is further refined through its binomial nomenclature, which is used to identify it as a unique species.
